- [lamalab-org/secs](https://github.com/lamalab-org/secs) — ⭐ 27 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Science — This is a machine learning framework called SECS, designed for the end-to-end elucidation of chemical structures from raw multimodal spectra like NMR, IR, MS/HSQC, combining contrastive learning and evolutionary algorithms.
- [yehlincho/absolute-stability-predictor](https://github.com/yehlincho/absolute-stability-predictor) — ⭐ 26 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Science — This repository contains code and models for accurately predicting the absolute stability of small protein domains. - [GT-KOALA/FFOLayer](https://github.com/GT-KOALA/FFOLayer) — ⭐ 22 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Training — FFOLayer introduces a novel fully first-order differentiable optimization layer for machine learning models, simplifying the backward pass in optimization. - [UIUC-MONET/vlm-circuit-tracing](https://github.com/UIUC-MONET/vlm-circuit-tracing) — ⭐ 22 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Multimodal — This repository provides code for "Circuit Tracing in Vision-Language Models," a research project presented at CVPR 2026, aimed at understanding the internal mechanisms of VLMs.
- [facebookresearch/VLM3](https://github.com/facebookresearch/VLM3) — ⭐ 17 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Multimodal — This repository provides the official code implementation for the research paper "VLM³: Vision Language Models Are Native 3D Learners", exploring how Vision Language Models can effectively learn from 3D data.
- [machinestein/Zero-Shot-Off-Policy-Learning](https://github.com/machinestein/Zero-Shot-Off-Policy-Learning) — ⭐ 16 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Training — This repository contains the official PyTorch implementation for the research paper "Zero-Shot Off-Policy Learning." It explores advanced reinforcement learning techniques focused on enabling models to perform tasks without prior direct experience in the target environment.

- [raphael-baena/morphology4metrology](https://github.com/raphael-baena/morphology4metrology) — ⭐ 12 · `Jupyter Notebook` · Image — This repository contains the official implementation of an architectural approach for the metrological analysis of historical scripts, utilizing morphological techniques for paleography research.

- [giannisanni/neutronstar](https://github.com/giannisanni/neutronstar) — ⭐ 18 · `C` · Infrastructure — NeutronStar is a CUDA-based fork designed to run giant Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models, like GLM-5.2 and Tencent Hy3, on a single consumer GPU. It achieves this by streaming experts from SSD, employing techniques such as io\_uring expert streaming, LFU host caching, cross-layer expert prefetching, and MTP speculative decoding to significantly improve inference speed.
- [oyama/pico-neural-amp-modeler-demo](https://github.com/oyama/pico-neural-amp-modeler-demo) — ⭐ 13 · `C` · Audio — This project demonstrates the real-time inference of the Neural Amp Modeler (NAM A2-Lite) on a Raspberry Pi Pico 2 (RP2350). It functions as a driverless USB audio device, showcasing how a neural network can process audio on embedded hardware.

</details>

## Cuda

- [xlite-dev/LeetCUDA](https://github.com/xlite-dev/LeetCUDA) — ⭐ 11,538 · `Cuda` · Infrastructure — LeetCUDA offers comprehensive and modern learning notes for CUDA programming with PyTorch, tailored for beginners. It includes over 200 CUDA kernels covering advanced topics like Tensor Cores, HGEMM, and FA-2 MMA.
- [Infatoshi/cuda-course](https://github.com/Infatoshi/cuda-course) — ⭐ 3,577 · `Cuda` · Other
- [mirage-project/mirage](https://github.com/mirage-project/mirage) — ⭐ 2,297 · `Cuda` · Infrastructure — Mirage is a persistent kernel project that compiles Large Language Models (LLMs) into a single, high-performance 'MegaKernel'. It aims to significantly increase LLM inference throughput by fusing the entire inference pipeline into a persistent GPU kernel, thereby eliminating scheduling overhead and CPU-GPU synchronization.
- [siboehm/SGEMM\_CUDA](https://github.com/siboehm/SGEMM_CUDA) — ⭐ 1,207 · `Cuda` · Infrastructure — SGEMM\_CUDA is a project that implements a fast single-precision general matrix multiplication (SGEMM) algorithm using CUDA from scratch. It serves as a demonstration and component for achieving high-performance matrix multiplication on GPUs in scientific and AI applications.
- [nv-tlabs/NKSR](https://github.com/nv-tlabs/NKSR) — ⭐ 944 · `Cuda` · 3D — NKSR (Neural Kernel Surface Reconstruction) is a research project, highlighted at CVPR 2023, focused on reconstructing 3D meshes from large-scale sparse point clouds using neural kernels.
- [uccl-project/mKernel](https://github.com/uccl-project/mKernel) — ⭐ 186 · `Cuda` · Infrastructure — mKernel is a collection of fast, multi-node, multi-GPU fused kernels designed to optimize compute and communication by integrating them into a single persistent GPU kernel. It covers both intra- and inter-node operations.
- [nerficg-project/faster-gaussian-splatting](https://github.com/nerficg-project/faster-gaussian-splatting) — ⭐ 166 · `Cuda` · 3D — This is an efficient and research-friendly framework for Gaussian Splatting, designed to improve the optimization process as detailed in the CVPR'26 paper "Faster-GS". It is used for novel view synthesis and 3D scene representation.
- [dataplayer12/SAM3-TensorRT](https://github.com/dataplayer12/SAM3-TensorRT) — ⭐ 142 · `Cuda` · Image — This repository provides an optimized NVIDIA TensorRT engine for Meta AI's SAM-3 (Segment Anything Model) to enable high-performance inference.
- [BlinkDL/Albatross](https://github.com/BlinkDL/Albatross) — ⭐ 110 · `Cuda` · Models — Albatross refers to an implementation related to the RWKV-7 G1g LLM, notable for its high-performance decoding capabilities.
- [SakanaAI/sparser-faster-llms](https://github.com/SakanaAI/sparser-faster-llms) — ⭐ 54 · `Cuda` · Infrastructure — This repository contains CUDA kernels designed to exploit sparsity in Large Language Models, aiming to enhance throughput and reduce memory footprint during both inference and training phases.
